question_text: Why do objects fall towards Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: Because they are heavier than air

options.label: B
options.text: Because Earth's gravity pulls them downwards

options.label: C
options.text: Because air resistance pushes them down

options.label: D
options.text: Because objects naturally move in circles

correct_answer: Because Earth's gravity pulls them downwards
explanation: Gravity is a force that attracts all objects with mass towards each other. Earth has a large mass, so it exerts a strong gravitational pull on smaller objects, making them fall downwards (towards Earth's centre).
